title: Persistent VM warm streaming, restored-snapshot warm-start, and distillation trace capture
origin: Authored as a missing MT-203 follow-on contract. MT-203 explicitly preserves 'MT-207 teacher/student distillation parallel (spec 9) on the coordinator'. The operator-selected repair scope also includes persistent VM warm streaming + warm-start from restored snapshot. This contract preserves both by making warm VM streaming the base runtime scope and limiting distillation/trace work to the MT-203-preserved teacher/student follow-on.
goal: Upgrade the VM-local model path from wave-1 ephemeral capture-then-chunk inference to a persistent, snapshot-restorable warm VM session that can stream tokens from a restored model-loaded guest, while capturing teacher/student traces needed for the MT-203-preserved distillation follow-on without making training claims prematurely.

base_scope.summary: Implement persistent VM warm streaming and warm-start from restored snapshots for swarm model sessions, then capture trace bundles suitable for later teacher/student distillation review.
included: Design and implement a persistent in-VM command channel for warm model sessions, preferably vsock guest-agent or a deliberately bounded serial daemon, with explicit protocol versioning and health checks.
Load a local model once inside a persistent Tier3Microvm, snapshot the model-loaded VM, and restore it as a warm session for later generate calls without full model reload.
Stream tokens or token-like chunks from the running guest to the host through the persistent channel with cancellation, timeout, terminal status, and backpressure.
Wire warm VM sessions into SwarmCoordinator/ProductionModelSessionFactory as a distinct runtime mode so ephemeral sandbox inference remains available and honest.
Expose snapshot lifecycle operations: create warm snapshot, restore warm session, invalidate snapshot, reclaim warm VM, and report warm-start latency.

Capture route/session/generation traces needed for the MT-203-preserved teacher/student distillation parallel follow-on: prompt, local output, cloud/escalated output, route reason, confidence/outcome, model ids, and validation labels where available.
Keep distillation as trace capture and queue preparation unless existing spec 9 surfaces already provide a training/evaluation pipeline to call safely.
non_goals: No fake claim that ephemeral exec provides true live per-token streaming.
No distillation model training unless an existing distillation pipeline and proof gates from MT-119..MT-124 are explicitly available and invoked through their public API.
No unsafe GPU passthrough for untrusted agents; GPU warm VMs require reviewed/trusted pinned artifacts per research risk.
No cloud escalation policy changes; MT-206 owns route semantics.

expected_diff_shape: Product implementation should touch sandbox/cloud_hypervisor guest-channel support, SandboxModelRuntime warm mode, WorktreeVmRegistry/snapshot registry, swarm production factory runtime modes, event/trace capture, distillation trace queue adapters, and focused fake + live ignored tests. The governance repair only authors this contract.
reuse: WP-KERNEL-004-sandbox-vm-execution-research-basis CloudHypervisorAdapter snapshot/restore, clone-safety, and explicit warning that persistent exec currently fails closed pending guest-agent work.
Cloud Hypervisor snapshot/restore and vsock research cited in the sandbox VM research basis.
MT-203 SwarmCoordinator lifecycle, leases, cancellation, budget, and event sink.
MT-206 route/correlation/idempotency records for local/cloud comparison traces.
Existing distillation/spec 9 modules and MT-119..MT-124 outputs where present.
Flight Recorder and session_checkpoint for durable trace bundles.
gaps_closed: Closes the honest performance gap where VM-local generate reloads the model every call.
Closes the streaming honesty gap by creating a real persistent guest channel instead of post-hoc chunking a completed stdout buffer.
Closes warm-start recovery gap by making restored snapshots usable as live inference sessions, not only snapshot artifacts.
Closes distillation-data gap by capturing aligned local/cloud teacher/student traces from real coordinator runs.
Makes future distillation work cheaper by storing trace bundles with route and validation context instead of raw transcripts only.

high_roi_additions.item: Warm-start latency metrics and cold-vs-warm comparison event
high_roi_additions.why_high_roi: The warm path is being built; measuring restore/load latency proves whether it is worth keeping.
high_roi_additions.addresses: Hidden performance regressions and unjustified complexity.
high_roi_additions.validation: Test emits cold_start_ms, restore_ms, first_token_ms, and warm_speedup_ratio fields.

high_roi_additions.why_high_roi: Snapshot handling is already in scope and a manifest prevents stale model/config mismatch.
high_roi_additions.addresses: Restoring a snapshot for the wrong model hash, runner version, or prompt protocol.
high_roi_additions.reuse: ModelRegistration sha256, SnapshotRef, worktree_id, runtime_binding metadata.
high_roi_additions.validation: Mismatch test rejects restore before booting guest.

high_roi_additions.why_high_roi: The stream parser is being built; redaction there prevents leaking secrets into traces.
high_roi_additions.addresses: Trace capture containing provider keys or operator secrets.
high_roi_additions.reuse: Existing FR redaction and terminal capture patterns.
high_roi_additions.validation: Trace test injects secret-like values and asserts redacted FR/distillation bundle.

high_roi_additions.item: Teacher/student trace quality gates before queueing
high_roi_additions.why_high_roi: Trace capture is cheap to validate before storage and prevents bad training data from accumulating.
high_roi_additions.addresses: Distillation queue pollution from failed, truncated, or policy-blocked sessions.
high_roi_additions.reuse: Distillation review modules, route outcome, session checkpoint terminal state.
high_roi_additions.validation: Only successful or explicitly labeled failure traces enter the distillation candidate queue.

risks_and_concerns.risk: Persistent warm streaming is faked by chunking completed stdout.
risks_and_concerns.plausible_failure_scenario: Implementation keeps ephemeral exec, splits stdout into chunks, and labels it warm streaming.
risks_and_concerns.hardening: Warm mode must use a persistent guest channel with observable first-token/stream events from a running restored VM. Ephemeral capture-then-chunk stays named as ephemeral mode.
risks_and_concerns.verification: Test proves the same restored guest handles multiple generate calls without full reload and emits stream frames before terminal completion.

risks_and_concerns.risk: Restored snapshots reuse cloned guest identity or stale secrets.
risks_and_concerns.plausible_failure_scenario: A model-loaded snapshot is restored into parallel sessions without clone-safety or reseed controls.
risks_and_concerns.hardening: Reuse the CH adapter clone-safety gate; require warm snapshot manifest; deny multiple live restores where identity cannot be reseeded; emit typed SnapshotCloneDenied.
risks_and_concerns.verification: Two concurrent restores of one snapshot produce one admitted and one typed refusal unless guest identity reseed is proven.

risks_and_concerns.risk: Guest agent becomes an unbounded command execution backdoor.
risks_and_concerns.plausible_failure_scenario: The persistent channel accepts arbitrary argv or shell commands from the host/app.
risks_and_concerns.hardening: Protocol supports only model-load/generate/cancel/health/shutdown verbs with schema validation, no shell, no arbitrary file paths outside declared binds, no network by default.
risks_and_concerns.verification: Protocol fuzz/negative tests reject unknown verbs, shell metacharacters, path traversal, and oversized payloads.

risks_and_concerns.risk: Warm VM leaks memory or survives cancellation.
risks_and_concerns.plausible_failure_scenario: A canceled stream leaves a restored VM and loaded model alive but invisible to the coordinator.
risks_and_concerns.hardening: Coordinator teardown owns guest shutdown, adapter kill, snapshot lease release, and ModelRuntimeState detach; lease reaper must reclaim warm sessions.
risks_and_concerns.verification: Cancel, timeout, app shutdown, and lease expiry tests assert kill/release/drop and ledger STOP parity.

risks_and_concerns.risk: Distillation trace capture stores bad or unsafe data as training candidates.
risks_and_concerns.plausible_failure_scenario: Failed local attempts, prompt echoes, or secret-bearing traces are queued as teacher/student examples.
risks_and_concerns.hardening: Trace bundle has status labels, redaction, route outcome, validation label, and queue eligibility gates. Training remains separate unless existing distillation gates are green.
risks_and_concerns.verification: Trace matrix tests assert failed/blocked/secret-bearing traces are either redacted and labeled or excluded from candidate queue.

risks_and_concerns.plausible_failure_scenario: Snapshot paths hardcode a drive letter, user profile, or WSL local path.
risks_and_concerns.hardening: Use configured runtime roots and relative metadata; absolute paths only in ignored machine-local config with documented relocation steps.
risks_and_concerns.verification: Portability test constructs snapshot manifests under a temporary runtime root with no hardcoded user path.

implementation_notes: The sandbox VM research basis explicitly says persistent VM exec is not available without guest-agent work. This MT is where that dependency becomes base scope, not a hidden assumption.
Warm VM mode must remain separate from the wave-1 ephemeral SandboxModelRuntime mode so validators can tell which path ran.
Every stream frame should carry sequence number, generation id, session id, and terminal reason; dropped frames require resync/error, not silent continuation.

Distillation/trace work is preserved only because MT-203 explicitly named MT-207 teacher/student distillation parallel. Keep it to trace capture and queue prep unless the existing distillation pipeline is proven ready.
Live CH tests may be ignored/env-gated, but headless fake adapter tests must prove protocol, lifecycle, redaction, and snapshot manifest behavior.
validator_focus: Warm mode is a real persistent restored-VM stream, not ephemeral stdout chunking.
Snapshot manifest prevents model/config/runner mismatch and hardcoded path drift.
Cancel/timeout/app-shutdown release every warm VM resource and ledger row.
Distillation trace capture is labeled, redacted, and eligibility-gated; no training claim is made without existing distillation proof.

lifecycle.readiness_note: Do not mark READY_FOR_VALIDATION without headless warm-protocol tests, snapshot manifest tests, trace redaction tests, and an explicit statement about whether the live CH ignored test was run.

governance_notes: Governance repair 2026-06-02 reconciled packet.json so MT-207 is declared as a current follow-on contract.
packet.json keeps MT-204 as the active/claimable next follow-on; MT-207 remains PENDING until its MT-204/MT-206 prerequisites are ready or a later operator-directed sequencing change occurs.

Distillation/trace scope is included only because MT-203 explicitly preserved teacher/student distillation parallel as MT-207. This contract does not broaden distillation beyond trace capture and queue preparation unless existing distillation proof gates are available.

minimum_controls: Warm streaming cannot be satisfied by post-hoc stdout chunking.
Snapshot restore clone-safety is tested under concurrency.
Guest protocol rejects arbitrary command execution.
Trace capture is redacted and queue-eligibility gated.
